raw_consumer_statement: american express has given me the worst customer experience I have ever had thus far. Someone stole and used my XXXX XXXXXXXX credit card on XX/XX/year> and made two unauthorized transactions, one from XXXXXXXX XXXX for {$30.00} on the XXXX and another for {$500.00} on the XXXX of XXXX. I reported these unauthorized transactions as soon as I found out and instead of treating me like a valued customer, american express locked my account and demanded selfie verification. They wouldn't even allow me to report these as unauthorized transactions unless I do the selfie verification which should be illegal. I told them I will do notary instead and amex agreed and sent me a notary packet. I had to get the notary done twice and every time I uploaded it through american express " secure upload '' platform, I received a receipt that I sent it to american express in my email ( screenshot attached ) yet every time I called for an update, american express reps would lie and tell me they didn't receive anything and to upload It again. I got lucky one time i called and had a rep who had more authority who was able to report the transactions unauthorized for me, but then I got an email saying my fraud dispute was resolved for the {$30.00} but the {$500.00} could not be found, yet I am being forced to pay for an unauthorized transaction that allegedly can't be found? On top of that they're stacking up late fee charges and ruining my credit score. I dont see anything more clearly of a lie than that, and I will use this as evidence in court. I'm sick and tired of these games, its so unprofessional and annoying. I have a busy work life and lots of responsibilities, I don't have time to go back and forth, treat me with respect please, because I did everything that was asked of me. I have receipts showing I sent in the notary forms twice, I have receipts in my email showing I reported the unauthorized transactions and proof that American express was being deceitful in its claims that it can not locate the {$500.00} transaction, because it clearly shows on my credit card statements I just downloaded.
